content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

July 22, 1985


Shirley Wechsler, Treasurer
Friends of David Roberti
11783 Laurelcrest Drive
Studio City, CA 91604

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-85-159

Dear Ms. Wechsler:

QUESTION

You have asked what, if anything, you or Senator Roberti
should do as a result of press stories alleging that certain
campaign contributions to the Senator and other members of the
Legislature received may have actually come from sources
different from those reported on their campaign statements.

CONCLUSION

You should file amendments if you or the Senator know the
source of a contribution was inaccurately reported. If you do
not know whether or not the allegations in the press are true,
you or the Senator should make reasonable inquiry as to the
source of the contributions in question.

ANALYSIS

As you know, the true source of a contribution should always
be reported on a campaign statement. Both candidates and
campaign treasurers have an obligation to use all reasonable
diligence in determining the true source of contributions, and
are required by Commission regulations to:

Correct any inaccuracies and omissions in campaign
statements of which the candidate [treasure] knows,
and cause to be checked, and, if necessary, corrected,
any information in campaign statements which a person
of reasonable prudence would question based on all the

Ms. Shirley Wechsler
July 22, 1985
Page 2


     surrounding circumstances of which the candidate
[treasurer] is aware or should be aware by reason of
his or her duties under this regulation and the Act.

                                2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
                                18427(a)(5), (c)(4)

     If a question has been raised by the press as to whether the
source of a contribution has been accurately reported on a
particular campaign statement, the campaign statement in
question should be amended if you or the Senator know that the
contribution was reported incorrectly. If you do not know
whether the contribution was correctly reported, you should take
reasonable steps to inquire into the true source of the
contribution. If you (or the Senator) write to both the
contributor listed on the report and to the alleged source of
the contribution asking who the source of the contribution was,
you will have satisfied the Act's and the regulation's
requirement of reasonable inquiry. If both persons agree that
the contribution was incorrectly reported, you should amend your
statements, and, if you like, attach the letters to the
amendments. If even after writing to both persons, it is still
not clear who the true source of the contributions was, you are
not required to amend your campaign statements.  However, you
may want to file your letters and the answers, if any, with the
original statements, or you may send them to us to show that
you did make an inquiry. You are not required to amend your
statements on the basis of press reports.

     With respect to your request for information in our files,
we can, of course make available any previously published
information on this subject. However, at present we are not at
liberty to make any other information public.

     I hope this answers your questions.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        General Counsel

BAM:nwm

DAVID A. ROBERTI
Senate President Pro Tempore

August 31, 1985


Barbara A. Milman, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Ms. Milman:

In accordance with your communication of July 22, 1985
I have enclosed copies of contribution inquiry letters.

We will, of course, file responses with the FPPC and at
that time determine if amendments to our original campaign statements are warranted.

I trust that we have satisfied your requirements of
reasonable inquiry.

Sincerely,

Shirley Wechsler, Treasurer
Friends of Roberti


Enclosures

sw


Friends of Roberti • 11783 Laurelcrest Drive • Studio City, California 91604 • (213) 877-5835
NOT PRINTED OR MAILED AT PUBLIC EXPENSE

DAVID A. ROBERTI

Senate President Pro Tempore

August 15, 1985


W. Patrick Moriarty, Chairman
Pyrotronics Corporation
2349 West La Palma
Anaheim, California 92801

Dear Mr. Moriarty:

A contribution from Card Construction Company in the
amount of $2,500.00 was made to Friends of Roberti in
December, 1981.

Certain newspaper allegations infer that this donation may
have come from a source other than that indicated on the
check.

Please provide us with any specific information you have
pertaining to the true origin of this contribution.

Thank you for your cooperation in this matter.

Sincerely,

Shirley Wechsler
Shirley Wechsler, Treasurer
Friends of Roberti

sw

Friends of Roberti • 11783 Laurelcrest Drive • Studio City, California 91604 • (213) 877-5835
NOT PRINTED OR MAILED AT PUBLIC EXPENSE

DAVID A. ROBERTI
Senate President Pro Tempore

August 15, 1985


Mr. Richard Keith
Card Construction Company
16642 Construction Way West
Irvine, California 92714

Dear Mr. Keith:

A contribution from Card Construction Company in the
amount of $2,500.00 was made to Friends of Roberti in
December, 1981.

Certain newspaper allegations infer that this donation may
have come from a source other than that indicated on the
check.

Please provide us with any specific information you have
pertaining to the true origin of this contribution.

Thank you for your cooperation in this matter.

Sincerely,

Shirley Wechsler, Treasurer
Friends of Roberti


SW

Friends of Roberti ● 11783 Laurelcrest Drive ● Studio City, California 91604 ● (213) 877-5835
NOT PRINTED OR MAILED AT PUBLIC EXPENSE
